summary: Fixed Windows-specific Puppeteer/Chrome launch failures (invalid --no-zygote flag, wrong Chrome path, needed bundled-Chromium fallback) that crashed every scraper on Windows.
Fixed Nominatim/geocoding 429 rate-limit floods for Cecil County Library and LibraryMarket scrapers, plus an SSL cert error in LibraryMarket.
PG Parks scraper was using the wrong Firestore-style save path instead of Supabase, plus a geocoding bug.
Children's museums scraper needed date-extraction fixes, a switch to the source site's REST API, and URL corrections after the source sites changed.
Fixed geocoding, URL rot, and event extraction issues across three scrapers in one pass.
GA and KY state parks event extraction broke after their calendar pages changed.
Fixed two issues surfaced by the first Group 1 run after the eastern-region rollout.
Several LibCal/Communico state scrapers were registered without their export function actually existing, or missing from the registry entirely; added the missing exports and registry entries.
Fixed Pratt Library's ~980 request failures, FL State Parks geocoding, and a broken runner checkpoint path found in the July 1 run.
Fixed closure/shutdown notices being misreported, incorrect runner stats, and Windows Chrome path detection.
Fixed data-quality false positives, wrong category slugs, and dateless-event handling in the audit scripts.
Dateless-events cleanup script crashed on invalid day numbers (e.g. "June 51, 2026") instead of treating them as unparseable.
Worcester Public Library was registered on the wrong platform scraper (platform mismatch); Drupal-PA was failing silently with no error surfaced.
Fixed a broken Chromium launch path, an SPA rendering timeout, and redundant geocoding calls across several scrapers.
Invalid-date and duplicate-event skip counts were merged into one "skipped" counter, so invalid-date extraction bugs were misreported as ordinary duplicates. Split into separate counters.
Fixed OCLS date extraction pulling the wrong/unparseable date text.
SC Aquarium's URL changed and needed a longer timeout; Hampton's events moved into an iframe requiring frame-aware scraping; removed dead Communico-TN entries.
Fixed scraper-monitor Firestore crash and LibCal-NH crash/save-bug/selectors; moved Kelley Library to its correct platform scraper.
Date-extraction regex accepted calendar-widget chrome (day-grid cells, month/year pickers) as valid dates, silently rejecting ~91% of real events downstream as invalid-date.

Removed ~5,500 fabricated {city}library.org domains and repaired ~900 more across all 44 WordPress-{state} library scrapers (auto-generated seed data was never verified against real domains); restored 3 libraries wrongly flagged dead and fixed Birmingham AL's domain in follow-up passes.
scraper-summary.log formatting was inconsistent between the live per-scraper line and the end-of-run recap table (InvalidDate silently omitted when zero); unified into one formatter used by both.
URL rot sweep across 7 multi-venue scrapers: stale /events paths on real domains repaired via homepage-crawl probing, 6 genuinely dead YMCA/JCC domains removed.

logScraperResult() called with the wrong signature (one object instead of 3 positional args) crashed every run; the site also migrated to a BiblioCommons-style search portal requiring new selectors and date extraction.

Same-day events were always compared against midnight, so any event scraped later than 00:00 local looked already-past regardless of its real start/end time. Now re-checks against the actual start/end time before rejecting a same-day event.
A duplicate "new states" registry block silently overrode WordPress-RI's rotation group from 3 to 1 (and redundantly re-declared WordPress-MN); removed the accidental duplicates.

B&N rebuilt stores.barnesandnoble.com as a Next.js/MUI app: all 136 hardcoded store IDs are stale (some now silently resolve to a DIFFERENT physical store, e.g. our Dover-DE id now points to a real store in Eugene OR), and the old a[href*=/event/] extraction selector no longer matches anything. Diagnosed and deferred rather than patched selectors alone, since the scraper trusts our config's city/state/venue name rather than the live page - a selector-only fix would have started saving real events under the wrong store's location. Needs a real fix: rediscover current store IDs for all 136 targets, add a live JSON-LD BookStore cross-check (name/addressLocality/addressRegion) to skip any store where our config no longer matches, and rewrite extraction against the new .MuiCardContent-root card structure.

Completed the rewrite deferred earlier the same day. Instead of rediscovering 136 store IDs, found the site's own JSON locator API (location/change/{zip} then /events, both undocumented) which returns every event within ~40mi of a zip with its own live storeId/name/address/city/state per event - eliminating the stale-ID risk entirely since venue data now always comes from the live response, never our config. Replaced all Puppeteer DOM-scraping with plain fetch(); STORES now used purely as search-point zips, deduped by eventId across overlapping searches, filtered to ACTIVE_STATES. Verified live: RI+MA test run saved 272 events, 0 errors, 0 geocode failures.

The dedicated MacaroniKid runners (split out from local-scraper-runner.js in the April 2026 separation) built their scraper list from MACARONI_SCRAPERS filtered only by group, never calling isScraperActive/getActiveStates - so all 43 state files ran every rotation regardless of region-config.json, even though the non-Macaroni path already used getScrapersForGroupByRegion for exactly this. Added the same active-state filter to all three runners; verified via --dry-run that inactive-region states (CA, TX, WA, CO, etc.) now correctly drop out of each group's run list.

One-time cleanup following the macaroni-runner region-filter fix: deleted 4,481 pre-existing out-of-region MacaroniKid events (CA, MN, IA, HI, NE, SD, AZ, OR, LA, KS, AR, TX, CO, WA, MO, ID, AK - states in neither scraper region-config.json nor the frontend's ACTIVE_STATES list, so zero site visibility either way). Left 1,486 OH/IN/MI/IL/WI MacaroniKid events alone since those states ARE in the frontend's ACTIVE_STATES and are currently visible on the live site - deleting them was not this task's call to make unilaterally. Also deleted 38 borderline-review events (non-family: winery/wine-festival tastings, 18+ adult rec registrations, pub psychic/murder-mystery/quiz nights, brewery karaoke, drag bingo at a distillery) flagged by cleanup-nonfamily-events.js Tier 3 pattern matching and manually reviewed against fetched descriptions.

0 events found in 3177s (53min) every run despite the feed domains being healthy (fast 200s). Root cause: geocodeAddress() was a local ad-hoc axios call to Nominatim with zero delay, zero timeout, and no shared rate-limit cooldown, hammering Nominatim on every event across 60 dates x 2 libraries and hitting sustained 429s. Swapped both call sites to the shared, cached, rate-limited geocodeWithFallback() from helpers/geocoding-helper.js. Verified live: 3 dates now complete in 38s with zero 429s (Photon resolving real branch addresses), extrapolating to ~12-13min for the full 60-day run instead of 53min.

0 events found every run despite the site rendering fine under Puppeteer (past a Cloudflare JS challenge). The site now wraps each event in two <a href=/calendar/event/ID> tags - one around a thumbnail image with empty text, one around the real title - and the extraction's processedUrls dedup marked the href consumed on the first (empty-text) match, silently discarding the real title link for every single event. Fixed by checking for a non-empty title before consuming the href. Also swapped the same unrate-limited/uncached geocodeAddress() pattern as Drupal-Pennsylvania to the shared geocodeWithFallback() helper, since this scraper will now actually process real events every run. Verified live: page 1 now finds 20 events (was 0) and saves successfully.

Removed as a duplicate: mdpls.org/events is already scraped successfully by Communico-FL (520 found/345 new in recent runs) via the real Communico API. This bespoke scraper fought a client-side React/MUI widget whose JS crashes under Puppeteer (Uncaught RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded), so the events-grid never left its Loading events... placeholder; the DOM elements it did match instead captured the calendar's Focused day label text as every event's date, so all 7 extracted rows were misdated to today with no time and rejected as past. Removed from scraper-registry.js; file left on disk unregistered.

Removed three dead single-library registry entries that always logged a pointless 0 found. Communico-MA: the only MA library was already relocated to LibCal-MA years ago, leaving zero entries in this file's MA array. Communico-MS (Jackson-Hinds Library System) and LibCal-AL (Huntsville-Madison County Public Library): both source URLs are genuinely decommissioned (jhlibrary.libnet.info 302-redirects to google.co.uk; hmcpl.libcal.com 404s as no longer online), not transient outages - both libraries migrated to new platforms (Drupal 10 site for Jackson-Hinds, a React/MUI Intercept widget for Huntsville) that need a properly verified new scraper, not a guessed DOM patch. Disabled the source entries with explanatory comments and removed the now-dead registry entries/functions rather than leave them silently logging 0 every rotation. AL and MS both retain other library coverage (WordPress-AL/Communico-AL, WordPress-MS).

User asked to verify the two dead-entry removals from earlier today actually had replacement coverage before treating them as resolved. Communico-MS (Jackson-Hinds): confirmed - live-tested WordPress-MS's existing extraction against jhlibrary.org/events and it finds 23 real events. No action needed. Communico-MA (Worcester Public Library): the removal was WRONG - it trusted a stale comment claiming Worcester had moved to LibCal-MA, but LibCal-MA only ever had a TODO stub for it, never a real entry, so Worcester had zero coverage. Found the library's real live calendar at mywpl.libnet.info/events (same LibNet/Communico platform as other entries in this file) and re-added scrapeCommunicoMA with the correct URL. Verified live: 10 real events saved with correct branch-level geocoding, 0 errors.

Found while verifying Jackson-Hinds coverage: isJunkTitle()'s skip-link pattern only matched exact "Skip to content/main/navigation", missing common variants "Skip to main content" and "Skip to current day" seen on jhlibrary.org and other Drupal sites - these would have saved as garbage events. Broadened the regex to catch both variants; verified against a mix of junk and real titles.

Added a verify-before-archive convention to the diagnosis prompt after the Worcester incident above: before deleting a scraper/library entry as covered elsewhere, actually run the alleged replacement and confirm real event output for that specific library rather than trusting an existing comment; fully remove (not comment-out) entries confirmed dead with confirmed replacement coverage; explicitly flag as an open gap (not resolved) any entry disabled without a confirmed replacement.

Manually re-ran the two scrapers fixed earlier today; output showed paired lines like 'Skipping cancelled/closed event: X' immediately followed by 'success checkmark X', meaning cancelled/closed events (and by the same code path: past, non-family, adult-only, junk-title, dateless, invalid-date events) were correctly rejected at the DB layer but still logged as saved and counted in imported++ by the calling scraper. Root cause: the Firestore-compat db.collection().add() wrapper's silent-skip path and its real-insert success path both returned the same bare {id} shape, so the ~89 scrapers using this .add() pattern have no way to tell a rejected event from a real insert unless they check for a distinguishing field, which none of them do. Added skipped/skipReason fields to the silent-skip return and updated the three call sites already touched today (Drupal-PA x2, FreeLibrary-Philadelphia x2, Communico-multi-state x2) to check it and log/count accordingly. Verified directly: add() on a crafted cancelled-event doc now returns {skipped:true, skipReason}, a normal event still returns the plain success shape. NOT fixed: the same miscounting bug likely exists in the other ~85+ scrapers that call .add() without checking the return value - out of scope for this session, flagging for a future sweep.

Full sweep of the add()-miscounting bug found earlier today (Drupal-PA/FreeLibrary-Philadelphia/Communico), applied to every other scraper calling db.collection('events').add() without checking the result: 42 of the 43 MacaroniKid state files (scripted fix on an identical shared block; MD uses a different save path and was unaffected), 43 other library/festival/farm scrapers (scripted regex fix matching the common await-add-then-console.log-then-counter++ shape, tolerant of differing counter/field variable names), and 4 hand-fixed stragglers with nonstandard shapes: macaroni-kid-scraper.js and scraper-macaroni-nc-cloud.js (unregistered legacy variants, same simple pattern as the other MK files but no console.log), scraper-festivals-eastern-us.js (counter/log order reversed - registered and active), and scraper-farms-eastern-us.js (had an activity_id-FK-retry branch feeding a single downstream success log - registered and active, needed the addResult captured across both the initial attempt and the retry). All 89 modified files pass node -c. Verified live: CivicEngage-Libraries (0 real events, ran clean) and Intercept-Camden-NJ (6 real events saved, 4 correctly skipped via unrelated duplicate-check path, no false positives). Independently confirmed via a full-codebase regex scan that zero files still contain the vulnerable pattern. Also confirmed activities.add() is not affected - no scraper calls it and flattenActivity() has no matching skip-throw reasons.

Same miscounting bug class as the .add() sweep, found in the batch()/commit() path used by saveEventsWithGeocoding() and saveEventsSimple() (helpers/event-save-helper.js, shared by ~50 scrapers) plus 8 standalone scrapers calling db.batch() directly. batch.set() queues an operation and the caller increments saved/imported immediately, but the actual content check (past/cancelled/junk-title/non-family/adult-only/dateless/invalid-date) only happens later inside batch.commit(), which silently dropped rejected rows without telling the caller - so every commit-time rejection was still counted as saved. Fixed commit() to collect and return skippedReasons; updated both event-save-helper.js functions (4 commit call sites total) to net out saved/skipped using the existing categorized counters (pastEvent/noDate/invalidDate) where they map cleanly, generic skipped bucket otherwise. Fixed the 8 standalone batch()-calling scrapers individually: scraper-aarecparks-md.js, scraper-fairfax-parks-va.js, scraper-kidsoutandabout-dmv.js, scraper-montgomery-parks-md.js (active/registered), scraper-allegany-county-library-MD.js, scraper-kent-county-library-MD.js, scraper-ruth-enlow-library-MD.js, scraper-worcester-county-library-MD.js (already disabled as redundant duplicates by a prior session, fixed anyway for consistency - zero current production impact). Verified live: saveEventsWithGeocoding and saveEventsSimple both correctly returned saved:1/skipped:2 (and 1/1) for crafted cancelled-event test batches, matching actual DB row counts exactly; a live AARecParks-MD run completed cleanly with no regression.

Extended the same fix to the doc(id).set() single-write path (also silently returned bare undefined on both skip and success, same ambiguity as .add()) and the corresponding update-path callers: 44 MacaroniKid files' existing-event UPDATE branch (doc(existingDoc.id).set(eventDoc) then unconditional updated++ - separate code path from the .add()-based new-event branch already fixed in the earlier sweep; scraper-macaroni-md.js needed two manual fixes since its whole file uses a different structure than the other 43), scraper-port-discovery-md.js, and scraper-pratt-library-MD.js. Left scraper-festivals-eastern-us.js's analogous update-path alone - it already unconditionally buckets updates as skipped (not saved) for reporting, so it was never inflating a success count, just coarse-graining the skip reason; not the same bug class. Verified live: updating an existing DB row to cancelled content via .set() now correctly returns {skipped:true} and leaves the original row untouched instead of corrupting it or miscounting it as updated. All 57 files touched across both fixes pass node -c.
